title: Calculating weekly surplus
problem: Jade earns $\$1{,}100$ per week. Her weekly expenses are: rent $\$500$, groceries $\$110$, transport $\$75$, utilities $\$40$, and phone $\$25$. Calculate her weekly surplus.

steps.instruction: Subtract total expenses from weekly income.

title: Categorising expenses and calculating savings rate
problem: A household has a monthly income of $\$5{,}000$. Their monthly expenses include: rent $\$1{,}400$, car insurance $\$110$, health insurance $\$130$, internet $\$60$, phone $\$50$ (all fixed); groceries $\$450$, transport $\$180$, entertainment $\$220$, dining out $\$150$, clothing $\$100$, and personal care $\$150$ (all variable). Calculate the total fixed expenses, total variable expenses, and the monthly savings rate.

steps.instruction: Express the surplus as a percentage of income.

answer: Fixed expenses: $\$1{,}750$. Variable expenses: $\$1{,}250$. Monthly savings rate: $40\%$.

title: Saving an emergency fund
problem: Ryan earns $\$4{,}800$ per month. His total monthly expenses are $\$4{,}000$. He wants to build a 3-month emergency fund. How many months will it take at his current savings rate? If he also reduces his entertainment spending by $\$400$ per month, how many months will it then take?

steps.instruction: Calculate months at the current rate.
Calculate the new surplus after reducing entertainment spending.
Calculate months at the new rate.

answer: At his current rate Ryan will take 15 months to save his emergency fund. By reducing entertainment spending by $\$400$ per month, he can reach his goal in 10 months — 5 months sooner.
